generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only identifiers (features, properties) visible in the AADL model when constructing AGREE contracts.
Ensure helper declarations (eq, const) use correct AGREE scalar types: int, real, or bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` helpers to define reusable expressions within AGREE contracts.
Range checks in AGREE typically use boolean expressions like `value >= lower_limit and value <= upper_limit`.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL elements (types, properties) solely to resolve AGREE errors.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within component implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to fix type mismatches by altering the AADL model; adjust helper declarations instead.
